name: Artichoke Hearts and Potatoes Cooked in Oil and Lemon
recipeIngredient: 4 Garlic cloves, peeled and lightly mashed
1 Whole lemon (3 tablespoons lemon juice + zest)
recipeInstructions:
1. Tie the fennel, peppercorns, coriander seeds, bay leaves, and garlic in a cheesecloth bundle.
2. Place the bundle in a 2-½ quart pot with 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
4. Remove the cheesecloth bundle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
5. Add lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and sugar. Mix well.
6. Halve the lemon and cut off any long artichoke stems.
7. Starting near the stem end of each artichoke, press back the leaves one by one and snap them off until past the pale inner leaves.
8. Using a sharp knife, remove the remaining leafy area and discard it. Rub all cut sections with half a lemon.
9. Scoop out the "choke" in the center of each artichoke using a grapefruit spoon. Squeeze lemon juice into this area and rub it in.
10. Trim the outside of each artichoke bowl, removing dark green sections until smooth. Rub these sections with lemon.
11. Cut each artichoke heart into four pieces, rubbing cut sections with lemon.
12. Peel and cut the potato into sections similar to the artichoke hearts.
13. Peel and cut the onion into eight sections.
14. Add the artichoke hearts, potatoes, and onion to the prepared liquid and bring to a boil.
15. Cover, lower heat, and simmer for about 12 minutes.
16. Taste one piece of potato and one piece of artichoke; adjust seasoning with salt or lemon if needed.
17. Continue simmering for another 3 to 7 minutes until vegetables are tender.
18. Remove the vegetables from the liquid using a slotted spoon before serving.
Source: Madhur Jaffrey's World-of-the-East Vegetarian Cooking
